data.title: Eos Energy prices debt, equity offering
data.description:
Eos Energy Enterprises (EOSE) has priced $525 million in 1.75% convertible senior notes due 2031 in a private offering to qualified institutional buyers.

The deal, increased from $500 million, is expected to close on November 24, 2025, subject to standard conditions. The company also gave initial purchasers a 13-day option to buy up to an additional $75 million in notes.

The notes will accrue interest at 1.75% per annum, payable semi-annually in arrears, and will mature on December 1, 2031, unless earlier repurchased, redeemed, or converted.

The initial conversion rate is 61.3704 shares of common stock per $1,000 principal amount of notes, representing an initial conversion price of ~$16.29 per share.

Eos estimates net proceeds of $507.9 million, or ~$580.5 million if the initial purchasers fully exercise their option to purchase additional notes. Net proceeds, together with the proceeds from a concurrent equity offering, if it is consummated, will be used to repurchase a portion of Eos’s outstanding 6.75% convertible senior notes due and for general corporate purposes.

Eos also announced the pricing of an offering of 35.85M shares of its common stock at $12.78 per share.

EOSE +3.5% premarket to $13.24

Source: Press Release

data.title: Shoe Carnival GAAP EPS of $0.53 in-line, revenue of $297.2M in-line; updates FY EPS outlook
data.description:
• Shoe Carnival press release (SCVL): Q3 GAAP EPS of $0.53 in-line.
  • Revenue of $297.2M (-3.2% Y/Y) in-line.
  • Comparable store sales declined 2.7 percent.

  • Fiscal 2025 Outlook

    The Company reaffirmed its Fiscal 2025 net sales outlook and updated its EPS outlook following strong third quarter results and accelerated rebanner execution. The Company now expects EPS for Fiscal 2025 in a range of $1.80 to $2.10 vs. $1.80 consensus, an increase in the lower end of the range of $0.10.

    Balance Sheet and Liquidity

    The Company ended third quarter 2025 debt-free, and cash, cash equivalents, and marketable securities totaled $107.7 million at quarter end, an increase of 18.2 percent compared to the prior year.

data.title: Allot beats top-line and bottom-line estimates; raises FY25 outlook
data.description:
• Allot press release (ALLT): Q3 Non-GAAP EPS of $0.10 beats by $0.06.
  • Revenue of $26.41M (+13.6% Y/Y) beats by $0.74M.
  • Net cash and cash equivalents, bank deposits, restricted deposits and investments as of September 30, 2025, total $81 million, an increase of $22 million versus $59 million cash and cash equivalents, bank deposits, restricted deposits and investment as of December 31, 2024. As of September 30, 2025, the company has no debt.
  • 60% YoY SECaaS ARR growth with robust profitability; raising full year guidance.
  • "Given the continued accelerated SECaaS growth, our solid visibility, and high level of backlog, we expect that our SECaaS ARR year-over-year growth will surpass 60%. We are raising our full year 2025 revenue guidance to between $100-103 million (vs. consensus of $99.80)."

data.title: Supermicro expands air-cooled AI solutions with AMD Instinct MI355X GPUs, stock up
data.description:
• Super Micro Computer (SMCI) on Thursday announced the latest addition to the AMD-based Instinct MI350 series GPU-optimized solutions, fueling gains in premarket trading.
  • Supermicro has designed this new system for organizations that need the high-end performance of AMD Instinct MI355X GPUs but require an air-cooled environment.
  • The new server delivers up to 4x generation-on-generation AI compute improvement and up to 35x leap in inferencing performance.
  • Super Micro Computer (SMCI) +5.4% at $35.55 premarket.

data.title: GDS targets nearly 300 MW new bookings for 2025 while advancing asset monetization through C-REIT
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: GDS Holdings Limited (GDS) Q3 2025

Management View

  • CEO William Huang reported "our revenue increased by 10.2%, and our adjusted EBITDA increased by 11.4% year-on-year, maintaining the healthy growth trend since our business began to recover last year." He noted the company's gross additional area utilized was around 23,000 square meters in the quarter, and stated, "We are on track to achieve our highest ever year of move-in." Huang highlighted that GDS is delivering the 40,000 square meter, or 152-megawatt order won in Q1, and has visibility for over 70,000 square meters of move-in from backlog next year. New bookings for the first nine months reached 75,000 square meters or 240 megawatts, and the company expects "to achieve nearly 300 megawatts for the full year, which is a big step-up from the level of the past few years."
  • Huang emphasized AI-driven demand, adding, "Around 65% of our bookings in 2025 are AI-related... All of our major customers are committed to the massive scale of this new investment cycle, with CapEx plans of hundreds of billions." He described the completion of the first IPO of a data center REIT in China as "a huge success," and said, "We intend injecting more assets in the REIT next year and establishing a continuous pipeline of asset monetization."
  • CFO Daniel Newman stated, "In 3Q '25, our reported adjusted EBITDA grew by 11.4% year-on-year." Newman explained, "On this pro forma basis, our adjusted EBITDA for the first 9 months grew by 15.4%." He noted, "Our C-REIT started trading on the Shanghai Stock Exchange on the 8th of August... as of yesterday's close, the C-REIT units were priced at RMB 4.375, 45.8% up from the IPO price." Newman added, "After 9 months, we are on track to achieve the midpoint of our revenue guidance and at or above the top end of our EBITDA guidance for the full year of 2025."

Outlook

  • Huang outlined, "We have unwavering confidence in the AI demand to come basis on the development and the ramp-up of domestic technologies. We believe that new bookings in the coming years could be better, and this is what we are preparing for in our strategic plan."
  • Newman said, "We are preparing assets with a target enterprise value of around RMB 4 billion to RMB 6 billion" for the first post-IPO asset injection into the C-REIT, compared with the IPO injection of RMB 2.4 billion. He added, "We still expect our organic CapEx for the full year to be around RMB 4.8 billion. However, net of the cash proceeds of the asset monetization, our CapEx will be around RMB 2.7 billion."
  • Management indicated, "Our growth rate during the current year has clearly benefited from the strong new bookings in 1Q '25... The relatively subdued new bookings since 2Q '25 will affect our growth rate next year. However, in our internal projections, we foresee higher bookings next year, leading to gross acceleration thereafter."

Financial Results

  • Newman reported, "Our net debt to last quarter annualized adjusted EBITDA multiple decreased from 6.8x at the end of 2024 to 6.0x at the end of 3Q '25. The decrease is mainly due to the cash proceeds of the asset monetization and the deconsolidation of debt of the project companies sold to the ABS and C-REIT as well as the offshore equity capital raise, which we did in 2Q '25."
  • He noted, "Our operating cash flow for the full year will be around RMB 2.5 billion. Therefore, after taking into account the asset monetization proceeds, our China business is almost self-funding."
  • The company disclosed, "We have already secured around 900 megawatts of powered land in and around Tier 1 markets, which is suitable for AI demand, particularly for AI inferencing."

Q&A

  • Yang Liu, Morgan Stanley, asked about the China market inflection and the aggressive approach in China. Huang responded, "Demand is very strong in China... our customer announced their big investment in the next 5 years... domestic chip is catching up... we still have the largest land bank -- powered land bank in and around Tier 1 market."
  • Yang Liu also asked about the new investment profile with the C-REIT. Newman answered, "The unit economics of the data center investment in China is very solid... we can generate typically 11% to 12% cash on cash yield on new investment... the IRR over a 5- to 6-year period, then it is in the low to mid-teens. And the levered IRR, the return on equity, is well into the 20s."
  • Xinyi Wang, UBS, inquired if higher bookings next year would include new powered land and about challenges in acquiring new power quota. Huang replied, "We're not fully relying on the new acquisition of the land. Definitely, we will -- if we can success to secure the land, power the land, we can do more... power quota always -- I mean, in general, always not easy... but... for us, it's not that challenge for us."
  • Frank Louthan, Raymond James, asked about DayOne funding and customer installs. Huang indicated DayOne is "fully independent... demand still remains very, very strong."
  • Michael Elias, TD Cowen, questioned gigawatt-scale demand and AI data center build times. Huang said, "Our client talk about gigawatt level... in China, we can build very fast... 9 months to 12 months is very normal... supply chain in China is not an issue."
  • Huiqun Li, BofA Securities, asked about AI exposure and government guidance. Newman stated, "We will probably reach nearly 300 megawatts in terms of new bookings for the whole of 2025... around 65% of the new bookings this year are AI related."
  • Timothy Zhao, Goldman Sachs, asked about pricing and competitive landscape. Newman said, "Over 2026, we expect the MSR to decrease by 3% to 4%." Huang added, "Now our customers more care about the financial capability, not just the capability you can build... we are much more way ahead than any competitor else."

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts maintained a positive tone, frequently congratulating management on solid results and focusing questions on growth, AI demand, and capital allocation.
  • Management's tone was confident and assertive, especially regarding AI-driven demand and capital market access, as evidenced by statements like "We have unwavering confidence in the AI demand to come..." and "we are more -- much more way ahead than any competitor else."
  • Compared to the previous quarter, management's confidence increased, particularly regarding AI demand and asset monetization, while analysts' optimism remained steady.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Management maintained revenue and EBITDA guidance in both quarters, but this quarter highlighted a more aggressive strategy for new business and accelerated asset monetization via the C-REIT.
  • Analysts' focus shifted from waiting for AI-driven demand in Q2 to detailed questions on AI bookings, land acquisition, and competitive advantages in Q3.
  • The tone of management's commentary was more assertive this quarter, emphasizing readiness for large-scale AI investments and improved access to capital.
  • Key metric changes included a 10.2% year-on-year revenue increase and a net debt to LQA adjusted EBITDA ratio of 6.0x vs. 6.1x in Q2.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management acknowledged challenges in acquiring new power quotas but cited "a lot of experience in the past -- in the last 10 years to build up the right relationship with the government and the power company."
  • Downward MSR reset remains a concern, with guidance for a 3% to 4% decrease in 2026.
  • Regulatory constraints on new power quota and land acquisition were highlighted, but management believes its experience and relationships offer mitigation.

Final Takeaway

GDS management emphasized robust AI-driven demand and positioned the company for aggressive expansion, leveraging a strengthened balance sheet, extensive powered land holdings, and the successful launch of its data center REIT. The company's strategic plan anticipates higher bookings and further asset monetization, with management highlighting strong customer commitments and an improving investment return profile. The outlook signals confidence in the company's ability to capture the next wave of AI infrastructure growth in China and maintain a leading market position.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Manner Coffee eyes an IPO as it takes on Luckin Coffee and Starbucks in China
data.description:
Chinese coffee chain giant Manner Coffee is now actively preparing for an initial public offering in Hong Kong that could arrive as early as 2026. The company aims to raise several hundred million U.S. dollars through the IPO, which reportedly could value the firm at $3 billion to $4.5 billion. Proceeds are expected to be used to further accelerate the chain's nationwide rollout and strengthen its competitive stance against larger, entrenched rivals such as Luckin Coffee (OTCPK:LKNCY), Starbucks (SBUX), Cotti Coffee, and Costa Coffee (KO).

Manner Coffee was founded in 2015 with a single, tiny roadside stall in Shanghai aimed at making premium coffee accessible to the city’s fast-growing base of young professionals. The boutique chain grew rapidly after it started to attract backers such as Chinese PE firm Capital Today, ByteDance (BDNCE), Singapore-based Temasek, and Meituan's (OTCPK:MPNGF) (OTCPK:MPNGY) venture arm. Today, Manner Coffee is extremely popular in China, especially among young urban professionals in top-tier cities. Most of the brand's stores are concentrated near business centers, subway transfer corridors, and shopping districts, making them highly accessible to younger workers with busy, fast-paced lifestyles.

The brand is also known for featuring lower coffee prices for comparable drinks at premium specialty chains in China. Manner is also known for its minimalist store design, affordable pricing, and a strong sustainability focus, offering discounts for customers who bring their own cups.

Looking ahead, the company is reported to be actively preparing to add as many as 400 stores in Indonesia in the coming year, viewing Southeast Asia as a strategic growth frontier. 

The big picture 

China now ranks as the world’s sixth-largest coffee market by total consumption and sales revenue, having more than doubled its demand over the past decade. China is just behind the United States, Germany, Japan, France, and Italy, and the rapid growth of coffee consumption is expected to push it even higher on the list in the next few years, which has made it a focus of investors looking for growth stories.

data.title: Cocoa slump helps chocolate bars, too late to save Christmas treats
data.description:
Cocoa futures have fallen to about $5,000/t, a 21-month low and more than 50% below the 2024 peak. Prices were trading 2.64% higher at $5,073.33 on Thursday.

Even so, cocoa remains historically elevated, still trading at roughly double its long-term average. The shift marks a decisive unwinding of an unprecedented supply squeeze and a broader normalization across the value chain, Ole Hansen, Head of Commodity Strategy at Saxo Bank, said.

After several years of weather disruptions, disease pressure, and ageing trees in Ivory Coast and Ghana, the world’s two dominant producers, the 2023–24 deficit pushed physical supply to breaking point.

"Crucially, farmgate prices lagged the futures spike, leaving farmers unable to invest just as climate volatility was intensifying. This mismatch created the conditions for the parabolic rise," Hansen added.

That dynamic is now reversing. Improved rainfall, better fertiliser use and rising producer-country prices have encouraged farmers to rehabilitate plantations, prune more aggressively and replant high-yielding varieties. Beyond West Africa, elevated returns have sparked investment in Latin America and Southeast Asia, gradually broadening the global supply base.

Yet, the current slump arrives far too late to affect Christmas assortments already produced and priced months ago, Saxo's Hansen said.

 

 

The supply shock hit during the production cycle for 2024 holiday products, meaning consumers will still face high prices and—depending on the brand—lighter bars with more palm oil than they might expect.

"But if the market stabilises around current levels, the impact could show up in 2026’s Easter eggs and bunnies," he added.

Overall, the slump has stabilised the market, given farmers breathing room, and eased pressure on buyers. For consumers, the benefits are coming—just not in time to salvage this year’s Christmas stockings.  

Relevant tickers include Mondelez International (MDLZ), Olam International (OTCPK:OLGPF), Nestlé (OTCPK:NSRGY), Conagra Brands (CAG), Simply Good Foods (SMPL), PepsiCo (PEP), Chocoladefabriken Lindt & Sprüngli AG (OTCPK:LDSVF), Barry Callebaut AG (OTCPK:BYCBF), J.M. Smucker Company (SJM), Hershey (HSY), Rogers Sugar (OTCPK:RSGUF), Rocky Mountain Chocolate Factory (RMCF), Costco (COST), Associated British Foods (OTCPK:ASBFY), and J&J Snack Foods (JJSF).

data.title: 'Vaccines do not cause autism' is not an evidence-based claim - CDC webpage
data.description:
A Centers for Disease Control and Prevention webpage now indicates that vaccines may cause autism, reversing its prior language on the matter, as "studies supporting a link have been ignored by health authorities."

"This webpage has been updated because the statement 'Vaccines do not cause autism' is not an evidence-based claim," it read. "Scientific studies have not ruled out the possibility that infant vaccines contribute to the development of autism."

The webpage noted that the Department of Health and Human Services launched a comprehensive assessment of the causes of autism, evaluating plausible biologic mechanisms between early childhood vaccinations and autism. 

"Mechanisms for further investigation include the impacts of aluminum adjuvants, risks for certain children with mitochondrial disorders, harms of neuroinflammation, and more," it added.

In addition, the header "Vaccines do not cause autism" was not removed from the page because of an agreement with the chair of the Senate Health, Education, Labor, and Pensions Committee that it would remain on the CDC website.

"Studies have shown that there is no link between receiving vaccines and developing autism," the webpage previously stated, citing a 2012 National Academy of Medicine review of scientific papers and a 2013 CDC study. 

The page now says the rise in autism prevalence since the 1980s correlates with the rise in the number of vaccines given to infants. "Correlation does not prove causation, but it does merit further study."

The webpage's content had come up during Health Secretary Robert F. Kennedy Jr.’s Senate confirmation process earlier this year. At the time, Sen. Bill Cassidy (R-LA) said RFK Jr. pledged that the CDC "will not remove statements on their website pointing out that vaccines do not cause autism."

RFK Jr. has also claimed that there may be a link between autism and pregnant women taking Tylenol, and even circumcision. Scientific consensus does not support these claims.

The webpage revision shows that the "CDC cannot currently be trusted as a scientific voice," Demetre Daskalakis, who previously served as the agency's top respiratory illness and immunization official, told The Washington Post.

Daskalakis, along with three senior CDC officials, resigned in August over the politicization of science at the agency.
